name: Margaret Torres
household: Lives alone in a rented apartment in South Austin; sister Carmen Reyes nearby, adult son Luis in Houston
goals: Stay independent in her apartment as long as safely possible, with practical support from family
Pay down $32K in combined credit card and medical debt before retirement
Build retirement savings past the current $47K — explore catch-up contributions and delay Social Security
Keep Parkinson's progression slow through exercise, medication, and therapy
Maintain daily routines using timers, checklists, and adaptive tools so tasks feel manageable
pain_points: Right-hand resting tremor makes typing, writing, and touchscreens extremely difficult
Financial anxiety — medical costs keep rising while income is fixed and retirement savings are far behind
Medication side effects (dizziness, nausea, restless legs) that vary day to day
Guilt about relying on sister Carmen for errands, bills, and paperwork
Fear of disease progression — losing the ability to drive, work, or live alone

personality.communication_style: warm, direct, and practical; prefers short concrete sentences over long explanations
personality.privacy_sensitivity: medium — cautious about health and financial details but trusts her care team and family
personality.ai_assistant_tone: patient, step-by-step, never condescending; keep instructions short and repeat key actions
strengths: good sense of humor about hard days
community-oriented — shows up for others at church and support group
adapts well when given the right tools and routines
growth_areas: building confidence with voice-to-text and phone reminders
advocating for herself at medical appointments instead of going blank
not catastrophizing about the future on bad tremor days

ai_tools: New to AI; tried a chatbot a few times with Carmen's help for medical and financial questions
productivity: voice-to-text for messages (set up by OT therapist)
social: email with family and medical offices (mostly receives, rarely sends)
phone calls with Luis and Carmen
tech_usage.frequency: Uses phone daily for calls and alarms; email a few times per week; typing is rare and difficult

presentation: right-hand resting tremor (dominant hand) — worst in afternoons when medication wears off
mild rigidity in right arm, especially in cold weather or after exertion
slowness of movement (bradykinesia) affecting fine motor tasks
occasional leg restlessness at night (medication side effect)
supports_used: Rock Steady Boxing (weekly/biweekly — balance, strength, confidence)
physical therapy biweekly (gait, balance, fall prevention)
occupational therapy monthly (grip tools, voice exercises, adaptive tech, tremor strategies)
Parkinson's support group monthly (St. David's Community Room)
weighted utensils, non-slip mats, fat-grip pens, weighted wrist cuffs for meals
voice-to-text and Siri reminders on phone

support_network.name: Carmen Reyes
support_network.role: weekly help with errands, bills, paperwork, and emotional support

support_network.name: Luis Torres
support_network.role: phone calls, occasional visits from Houston, tech help, emotional anchor

support_network.name: Dr. Patel
support_network.role: Parkinson's management, medication adjustments, tremor monitoring

support_network.name: Linda Chen
support_network.role: adaptive tools, voice exercises, tremor strategies, tech setup

support_network.name: Maria Davis
support_network.role: debt reduction plan, retirement planning, budget management

support_network.name: Helen
support_network.role: mutual support, shared experience with Parkinson's

routine_anchors: Rock Steady Boxing at YMCA East Austin
Parkinson's support group at St. David's
part-time work shifts at Henderson & Briar Insurance
monthly financial counseling at GreenPath Financial
